message:
feat(ui): add ConfigDisplay for rich configuration display (bd-3q6w)

Add ConfigDisplay component that renders configuration with rich formatting:
- Hierarchical tree view of configuration sections
- Source indicators for each value when show_sources is enabled
- All config sections: general, compilation, transfer, circuit, output
- Sources summary showing priority order

Features:
- Rich mode: uses rich_rust Panel with tree-structured content
- Plain mode: TOML-like sections for non-interactive terminals
- Machine mode: suppresses output (caller uses print_json instead)
- Optional source annotations showing where each value comes from

Sections displayed:
- [general]: enabled, log_level, socket_path
- [compilation]: confidence_threshold, min_local_time_ms
- [transfer]: compression_level, exclude_patterns
- [circuit]: failure_threshold, success_threshold, error_rate_threshold, etc.
- [output]: visibility, first_run_complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3n00","title":"Create Unit Tests for Test Harness & Utilities","description":"## Purpose
Create comprehensive unit tests for the test harness itself, ensuring the test infrastructure is reliable before using it to test rch.

## Why This Matters
- A buggy test harness produces false positives/negatives
- Harness bugs are harder to debug than rch bugs
- Unit tests catch harness issues before they affect e2e tests
- Provides documentation of expected harness behavior

## Test Categories

### 1. Logging Infrastructure Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/logging_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_log_entry_is_valid_json() {
    let logger = TestLogger::new("test_example");
    logger.info("test message", &[("key", "value")]);
    let log_content = std::fs::read_to_string(logger.log_path()).unwrap();
    for line in log_content.lines() {
        serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line).expect("invalid JSON");
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_timing_accuracy() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_log_levels_filter_correctly() { ... }
```

### 2. Output Comparison Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/output_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_identical_outputs_compare_equal() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_path_normalization() {
    let local = "/home/user/project/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let remote = "/tmp/rch/project_abc/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let normalizer = OutputNormalizer::default();
    assert_eq!(normalizer.normalize(local), normalizer.normalize(remote));
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_shows_actual_differences() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_binary_comparison_exact() { ... }
```

### 3. Worker Discovery Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/discovery_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_workers_available_returns_false_when_no_config() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_skip_mechanism_logs_reason() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_health_check_timeout() { ... }
```

### 4. Harness Lifecycle Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/harness_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_daemon_spawn_and_cleanup() {
    let harness = TrueE2EHarness::new_or_skip().unwrap();
    // Daemon should be running
    assert!(harness.daemon_is_healthy());
    drop(harness);
    // Daemon should be cleaned up (if harness spawned it)
}

#[test]
fn test_fixture_isolation() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_project_cleanup() { ... }
```

### 5. Timing Utilities Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/timing_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_measure_command_timing_accuracy() {
    let start = Instant::now();
    let measured = measure_command_timing("sleep 0.1");
    let actual = start.elapsed();
    // Should be within 10ms of actual
    assert!((measured.as_millis() as i64 - actual.as_millis() as i64).abs() < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_percentile_calculation() { ... }
```

## Test Organization
```
tests/
└── true_e2e/
    ├── mod.rs
    ├── harness.rs
    ├── logging.rs
    ├── output.rs
    └── tests/           # Unit tests for harness
        ├── mod.rs
        ├── logging_tests.rs
        ├── output_tests.rs
        ├── discovery_tests.rs
        ├── harness_tests.rs
        └── timing_tests.rs
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% code coverage for logging module
- [ ] All output comparison edge cases tested
- [ ] Worker discovery tests run without real workers
- [ ] Harness lifecycle tested end-to-end
- [ ] All tests pass with `cargo test` (no workers needed)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:54.582781639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:18.952672726Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:18.952614737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-1tq2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:16.906775866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-3r2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:17.872745681Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:15.663722245Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ndq","title":"Implement JobLifecycleLog for structured job event display","description":"Create JobLifecycleLog formatter in rchd/src/ui/jobs.rs for job event visualization:
- Job submission: job ID, source, command summary, assigned worker
- Job progress: compilation phase, elapsed time, resource usage
- Job completion: success/failure indicator, duration, artifact count
- Job failure: error type, worker state, suggested remediation

Technical requirements:
- Prefix each line with timestamp and job ID for log grep-ability
- Use consistent column alignment for scanability
- Color code by event type: blue (start), yellow (progress), green (success), red (failure)
- Include Unicode status icons inline
- Rate-limit progress updates (max 1/second) to prevent log spam
- Support both single-line compact and multi-line detailed modes

Format examples:
[14:30:01] [j-a3f2] ● START cargo build --release → worker1
[14:30:15] [j-a3f2] ◐ BUILD 14.0s │ Compiling foo v0.1.0 (45/120 crates)
[14:30:45] [j-a3f2] ✓ DONE  44.2s │ 156 artifacts │ cache written
[14:30:46] [j-b7c1] ✗ FAIL  12.1s │ worker2 timeout │ retry queued","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:49.774732595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:56.340481977Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ndq","depends_on_id":"bd-1z6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:49.787981059Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ndq","depends_on_id":"bd-3u68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:56.340431592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3obh","title":"CRITICAL: Compile Command Hook Context Test","description":"## Test: rch compile in Hook Context

This test verifies that when an AI agent invokes `rch compile` (or any compilation command), the rich_rust integration does NOT interfere with the compilation output that the agent is parsing.

### Why This Is Critical

When Claude Code uses RCH, it:
1. Invokes `rch hook --stdin` with JSON describing the command
2. Expects JSON response with `allow`/`intercept` result
3. If intercepted, expects compilation stdout/stderr to pass through unchanged
4. Parses exit codes to determine success/failure

If rich output contaminates stdout, agents will fail to parse compilation results.

### Test Scenarios

#### Scenario 1: Hook Invocation (JSON Protocol)
```bash
# Test: Hook JSON response unchanged
INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"cargo build"}}'
OUTPUT=$(echo "$INPUT" | rch hook --stdin)

# Verify: stdout is valid JSON
echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e . || fail "Not valid JSON"

# Verify: No ANSI codes in JSON
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qP '\x1b\[' && fail "ANSI codes in JSON!"
```

#### Scenario 2: Compilation Output Passthrough
```bash
# Test: cargo build output unchanged
cd /tmp/test_project
OUTPUT=$(rch compile cargo build 2>&1)

# Verify: rustc output preserved
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Compiling" || echo "WARN: No Compiling output"

# Verify: Exit code preserved
rch compile cargo build
EXIT=$?
[[ $EXIT -eq 0 ]] || [[ $EXIT -eq 101 ]] || fail "Unexpected exit: $EXIT"
```

#### Scenario 3: Error Output Preserved
```bash
# Create invalid Rust file
echo "fn main() { invalid syntax" > /tmp/test_project/src/main.rs

# Compile and capture error
OUTPUT=$(rch compile cargo build 2>&1)

# Verify: rustc error preserved
echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "error" || fail "Error not preserved"

# The error message itself should be parseable by agents
# Rich formatting should be on stderr, not mixed with compiler output
```

### Test Implementation

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test_compile_hook_context.sh
set -euo pipefail

source scripts/test_lib.sh
init_test_log "compile_hook_context"

RCH="./target/release/rch"
TEST_PROJECT="/tmp/rch_test_$$"

setup() {
    log_json "setup" "Creating test project"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_PROJECT/src"
    cat > "$TEST_PROJECT/Cargo.toml" << 'EOF'
[package]
name = "test_project"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    echo 'fn main() { println!("Hello"); }' > "$TEST_PROJECT/src/main.rs"
}

cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_PROJECT"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

setup

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 1: Hook invocation JSON purity
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Hook JSON purity test"

export RCH_HOOK_MODE=1
cd "$TEST_PROJECT"

HOOK_INPUT='{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"cargo build"}}'
STDOUT_FILE="$(mktemp)"
STDERR_FILE="$(mktemp)"

echo "$HOOK_INPUT" | "$RCH" hook --stdin > "$STDOUT_FILE" 2> "$STDERR_FILE"

# Check stdout
if ! jq -e . "$STDOUT_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "stdout is not valid JSON"
fi

if grep -qP '\x1b\[' "$STDOUT_FILE"; then
    log_json "error" "ANSI codes found in stdout" "{\"content\":\"$(cat $STDOUT_FILE | head -c 500 | base64)\"}"
    fail "ANSI codes in hook stdout!"
fi

log_json "verify" "Hook stdout is clean JSON" '{"passed":true}'
unset RCH_HOOK_MODE

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Compilation stdout/stderr separation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Compilation output separation"

# Run compile with rich output potentially enabled
STDOUT_FILE="$(mktemp)"
STDERR_FILE="$(mktemp)"

"$RCH" compile cargo build --release > "$STDOUT_FILE" 2> "$STDERR_FILE" || true

# stdout should have compiler output (or be empty if intercepted)
# It should NOT have ANSI codes from RCH (compiler may add its own)
RCH_ANSI_PATTERN='\[rch\].*\x1b\['  # RCH-specific rich output pattern
if grep -qP "$RCH_ANSI_PATTERN" "$STDOUT_FILE"; then
    fail "RCH rich output leaked to stdout"
fi

log_json "verify" "stdout clean of RCH rich output" '{"passed":true}'

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Exit code preservation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Exit code preservation"

# Successful build
"$RCH" compile cargo build --release >/dev/null 2>&1
EXIT_SUCCESS=$?

# Failed build (introduce error)
echo 'fn main() { invalid' > "$TEST_PROJECT/src/main.rs"
"$RCH" compile cargo build --release >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
EXIT_FAIL=$?

# Restore
echo 'fn main() { println!("Hello"); }' > "$TEST_PROJECT/src/main.rs"

log_json "verify" "Exit codes" "{\"success\":$EXIT_SUCCESS,\"fail\":$EXIT_FAIL}"

if [[ $EXIT_SUCCESS -ne 0 ]] && [[ $EXIT_SUCCESS -ne 101 ]]; then
    log_json "warning" "Unexpected success exit code" "{\"exit\":$EXIT_SUCCESS}"
fi

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 4: Error message preservation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "test" "Error message preservation"

echo 'fn main() { let x: i32 = "string"; }' > "$TEST_PROJECT/src/main.rs"
ERROR_OUTPUT="$(\"\\" compile cargo build 2>&1 || true)"

# Should contain rustc error (type mismatch)
if ! echo "$ERROR_OUTPUT" | grep -q "mismatched types\|expected.*found"; then
    log_json "warning" "Error message may be modified" "{\"output_sample\":\"$(echo $ERROR_OUTPUT | head -c 200)\"}"
fi

log_json "verify" "Error messages preserved" '{"passed":true}'

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SUMMARY
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log_json "summary" "All compile hook context tests passed" '{"total_tests":4}'
echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "ALL COMPILE HOOK CONTEXT TESTS PASSED"
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
```

### Acceptance Criteria

1. [ ] Hook JSON response contains no ANSI codes
2. [ ] Compilation stdout has no RCH rich output leakage
3. [ ] Exit codes preserved through rich integration
4. [ ] Compiler error messages preserved and parseable
5. [ ] Test runs in CI

### Files

- CREATE: scripts/test_compile_hook_context.sh

 {"id":"bd-3r1e","title":"Phase 4: Error Experience - Beautiful, Actionable Error Messages","description":"Transform error messages across all RCH components into beautiful, actionable displays. This phase covers: ErrorPanel renderable for all error types, categorized error styling (network, config, permission, timeout, internal), suggested remediation steps, error codes for documentation lookup, and context preservation for debugging. The goal is to make errors helpful rather than frustrating - every error should tell the user what went wrong, why, and how to fix it.

Design principles:
- Errors go to stderr always (machine agents parse stdout)
- Include error code (e.g., RCH-E001) for searchable documentation
- Provide specific remediation steps, not generic 'check your configuration'
- Show relevant context (which worker, which file, what was attempted)
- Use red for errors, yellow for warnings, cyan for suggestions
- Never hide stack traces from --debug mode
- Support machine-readable error format via --json flag","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:08:11.413643390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:08:11.429626603Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3r1e","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:08:11.429520954Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3r2b","title":"Implement Output Comparison Utilities","description":"## Purpose
Implement output comparison utilities that intelligently compare local vs remote execution output, handling acceptable differences like paths, timestamps, and ordering.

## Why This Matters
- Raw byte comparison would fail on acceptable differences (paths, timestamps)
- AI agents need to know exactly WHAT is different when tests fail
- Different comparison strategies needed for different output types

## Requirements

### Comparison Modes
1. **Exact**: Byte-for-byte identical (for binary artifacts)
2. **Normalized**: Ignore paths, timestamps, ordering (for compilation output)
3. **Semantic**: Exit code + key patterns only (for error checking)

### Normalization Rules
```rust
pub struct OutputNormalizer {
    /// Replace absolute paths with <PROJECT_ROOT> or <HOME>
    pub normalize_paths: bool,
    /// Strip timestamps like "2024-01-19 12:00:00"
    pub strip_timestamps: bool,
    /// Sort lines for parallel output comparison
    pub sort_lines: bool,
    /// Strip ANSI codes for content comparison
    pub strip_ansi: bool,
    /// Ignore whitespace differences
    pub ignore_whitespace: bool,
}
```

### Diff Generation
When outputs differ, generate a clear diff:
```
=== Output Comparison Failed ===
Expected (local):
  line 1: Compiling hello_world v0.1.0 (/home/user/project)
  line 2: Finished dev target(s) in 1.23s

Actual (remote):
  line 1: Compiling hello_world v0.1.0 (/tmp/rch/project_abc123)
  line 2: Finished dev target(s) in 0.98s

Diff (after normalization):
  (no differences after path normalization)
```

### Path Patterns to Normalize
- `/home/*/` → `<HOME>/`
- `/tmp/rch/` → `<RCH_WORK>/`
- `/Users/*/` → `<HOME>/` (macOS)
- Cargo target paths

### Timestamp Patterns to Strip
- ISO 8601: `2024-01-19T12:00:00`
- Build times: `in 1.23s`, `took 45ms`
- Unix timestamps

### Implementation

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/output.rs

pub struct OutputComparison {
    pub local: CapturedOutput,
    pub remote: CapturedOutput,
    pub normalizer: OutputNormalizer,
}

impl OutputComparison {
    pub fn compare(&self) -> ComparisonResult;
    pub fn diff(&self) -> String;
    pub fn normalized_diff(&self) -> String;
}

pub enum ComparisonResult {
    Identical,
    EquivalentAfterNormalization,
    Different { diff: String },
}

pub struct CapturedOutput {
    pub stdout: Vec<u8>,
    pub stderr: Vec<u8>,
    pub exit_code: i32,
    pub duration: Duration,
}
```

## MANDATORY Logging

All comparison operations MUST produce structured JSON logs for debugging and validation.

### Comparison Start
```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","component":"output_comparison","phase":"start","msg":"Starting output comparison","data":{"mode":"normalized","local_stdout_len":1234,"local_stderr_len":56,"remote_stdout_len":1230,"remote_stderr_len":60,"local_exit":0,"remote_exit":0}}
```

### Normalization Applied
```json
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","component":"output_comparison","phase":"normalize","msg":"Applying normalization rules","data":{"normalize_paths":true,"strip_timestamps":true,"sort_lines":false,"strip_ansi":false,"paths_replaced":12,"timestamps_stripped":3}}
```

### Path Normalization Details
```json
{"ts":"...","level":"TRACE","component":"output_comparison","phase":"normalize","msg":"Path normalization","data":{"original":"/home/ubuntu/project","normalized":"<HOME>/project","pattern":"home_dir"}}
```

### Comparison Result
```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","component":"output_comparison","phase":"complete","msg":"Comparison complete","data":{"result":"equivalent_after_normalization","mode":"normalized","stdout_match":true,"stderr_match":true,"exit_code_match":true,"duration_ms":5}}
```

### Comparison Failure Details
```json
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","component":"output_comparison","phase":"complete","msg":"Output comparison failed","data":{"result":"different","stdout_diff_lines":3,"stderr_diff_lines":0,"exit_code_match":true,"first_diff_line":15,"diff_preview":"- expected: Compiling foo...\n+ actual: error[E0433]..."}}
```

### Diff Generation
```json
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","component":"output_comparison","phase":"diff","msg":"Generating diff output","data":{"format":"unified","context_lines":3,"total_hunks":2,"additions":5,"deletions":3}}
```

### Binary Comparison
```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","component":"output_comparison","phase":"binary_compare","msg":"Binary artifact comparison","data":{"file":"target/debug/hello","local_size":123456,"remote_size":123456,"local_hash":"sha256:abc123...","remote_hash":"sha256:abc123...","match":true}}
```

### Error During Comparison
```json
{"ts":"...","level":"ERROR","component":"output_comparison","phase":"error","msg":"Comparison failed","data":{"error":"invalid utf-8 in output","local_is_utf8":true,"remote_is_utf8":false,"fallback":"binary_comparison"}}
```

## Unit Tests for Output Comparison

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/output_comparison_tests.rs

use crate::output::{OutputComparison, OutputNormalizer, CapturedOutput, ComparisonResult};
use std::time::Duration;

#[test]
fn test_exact_match_identical_output() {
    let output = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"Hello, World!\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local: output.clone(),
        remote: output,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::exact(),
    };

    assert!(matches!(comparison.compare(), ComparisonResult::Identical));
}

#[test]
fn test_path_normalization_makes_equal() {
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"Compiling at /home/ubuntu/myproject\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"Compiling at /tmp/rch/work_abc123\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(95),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::default().with_path_normalization(true),
    };

    assert!(matches!(comparison.compare(), ComparisonResult::EquivalentAfterNormalization));
}

#[test]
fn test_timestamp_stripping() {
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"Built in 1.23s at 2024-01-19T12:00:00Z\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(1230),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"Built in 0.98s at 2024-01-19T12:05:30Z\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(980),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::default().with_timestamp_stripping(true),
    };

    assert!(matches!(comparison.compare(), ComparisonResult::EquivalentAfterNormalization));
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_generation_shows_actual_difference() {
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"line1\nline2\nline3\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"line1\nmodified_line2\nline3\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::exact(),
    };

    let result = comparison.compare();
    assert!(matches!(result, ComparisonResult::Different { .. }));

    let diff = comparison.diff();
    assert!(diff.contains("-line2"));
    assert!(diff.contains("+modified_line2"));
}

#[test]
fn test_binary_comparison_detects_corruption() {
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: vec![0x7f, 0x45, 0x4c, 0x46, 0x01, 0x02], // ELF header
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: vec![0x7f, 0x45, 0x4c, 0x46, 0x01, 0x03], // Different byte
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::exact(),
    };

    assert!(matches!(comparison.compare(), ComparisonResult::Different { .. }));
}

#[test]
fn test_ansi_stripping_preserves_content() {
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"\x1b[32mSuccess\x1b[0m: compiled\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"Success: compiled\n".to_vec(), // No ANSI codes
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::default().with_ansi_stripping(true),
    };

    assert!(matches!(comparison.compare(), ComparisonResult::EquivalentAfterNormalization));
}

#[test]
fn test_parallel_output_sorting() {
    // Parallel builds may produce lines in different order
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"Compiling a\nCompiling b\nCompiling c\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"Compiling b\nCompiling c\nCompiling a\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::default().with_line_sorting(true),
    };

    assert!(matches!(comparison.compare(), ComparisonResult::EquivalentAfterNormalization));
}

#[test]
fn test_exit_code_mismatch_fails_comparison() {
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"output\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"output\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: vec![],
        exit_code: 1, // Different exit code
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::default(),
    };

    let result = comparison.compare();
    assert!(matches!(result, ComparisonResult::Different { .. }));
}

#[test]
fn test_stderr_comparison() {
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: vec![],
        stderr: b"warning: unused variable\n".to_vec(),
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: vec![],
        stderr: b"warning: unused variable\n".to_vec(),
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::exact(),
    };

    assert!(matches!(comparison.compare(), ComparisonResult::Identical));
}

#[test]
fn test_semantic_comparison_ignores_output_checks_exit() {
    let local = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"lots of different output here\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: b"various warnings\n".to_vec(),
        exit_code: 0,
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let remote = CapturedOutput {
        stdout: b"completely different text\n".to_vec(),
        stderr: b"other stuff\n".to_vec(),
        exit_code: 0, // Same exit code
        duration: Duration::from_millis(100),
    };

    let comparison = OutputComparison {
        local,
        remote,
        normalizer: OutputNormalizer::semantic(), // Only checks exit code
    };

    assert!(matches!(comparison.compare(), ComparisonResult::EquivalentAfterNormalization));
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3ri3","title":"Create Test Fixture: Rust Workspace Project","description":"## Purpose
Create a multi-crate workspace project fixture for testing workspace-level cargo commands (cargo build --workspace, cargo test --workspace, -p package filtering).

## Why This is Critical
bd-2kr0 and bd-10g8 explicitly test workspace scenarios:
- "Workspace build: cargo build --workspace"
- "Package-specific: cargo build -p specific_package"
- "Workspace tests: cargo test --workspace"

Without this fixture, these test cases CANNOT be implemented.

## Requirements
1. Multi-crate Cargo workspace under tests/e2e/fixtures/rust_workspace/
2. At least 3 member crates with dependencies between them
3. Both library and binary crates
4. Inter-crate dependencies to test incremental builds
5. Tests in each crate

## Structure
```
tests/e2e/fixtures/rust_workspace/
├── Cargo.toml              # Workspace manifest
├── crates/
│   ├── core/               # Library crate (foundational)
│   │   ├── Cargo.toml
│   │   └── src/lib.rs
│   ├── utils/              # Library crate depending on core
│   │   ├── Cargo.toml
│   │   └── src/lib.rs
│   └── app/                # Binary crate depending on core and utils
│       ├── Cargo.toml
│       └── src/main.rs
└── tests/
    └── workspace_integration.rs
```

## File Content Specifications

### Cargo.toml (workspace root)
```toml
[workspace]
members = ["crates/core", "crates/utils", "crates/app"]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.package]
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
authors = ["RCH Test Fixtures"]
license = "MIT"

# Shared dependencies across workspace
[workspace.dependencies]
# No external deps for this simple fixture
```

### crates/core/Cargo.toml
```toml
[package]
name = "workspace_core"
version.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true

[lib]
name = "workspace_core"

[dependencies]
# No external dependencies - this is the foundation crate

[dev-dependencies]
# No dev dependencies needed
```

### crates/core/src/lib.rs
```rust
//! Core library providing foundational types and functions.
//! This crate has no dependencies and is the base of the workspace dependency graph.

/// A simple configuration struct used across the workspace.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub struct Config {
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub debug: bool,
}

impl Config {
    /// Create a new configuration with the given name.
    pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            name: name.into(),
            version: env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").to_string(),
            debug: cfg!(debug_assertions),
        }
    }

    /// Returns a greeting message based on the config.
    pub fn greeting(&self) -> String {
        format!("Hello from {} v{}", self.name, self.version)
    }
}

/// Core computation function used by other crates.
pub fn compute(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 {
    a + b
}

/// Validates input according to core rules.
pub fn validate_input(input: &str) -> Result<String, &'static str> {
    if input.is_empty() {
        Err("Input cannot be empty")
    } else if input.len() > 100 {
        Err("Input too long")
    } else {
        Ok(input.to_uppercase())
    }
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_config_new() {
        let config = Config::new("test_app");
        assert_eq!(config.name, "test_app");
        assert_eq!(config.version, "0.1.0");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_config_greeting() {
        let config = Config::new("rch_fixture");
        let greeting = config.greeting();
        assert!(greeting.contains("rch_fixture"));
        assert!(greeting.contains("0.1.0"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_compute() {
        assert_eq!(compute(2, 3), 5);
        assert_eq!(compute(-1, 1), 0);
        assert_eq!(compute(0, 0), 0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_validate_input_success() {
        let result = validate_input("hello");
        assert_eq!(result, Ok("HELLO".to_string()));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_validate_input_empty() {
        let result = validate_input("");
        assert_eq!(result, Err("Input cannot be empty"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_validate_input_too_long() {
        let long_input = "x".repeat(101);
        let result = validate_input(&long_input);
        assert_eq!(result, Err("Input too long"));
    }
}
```

### crates/utils/src/lib.rs
```rust
//! Utility functions that build on top of workspace_core.
//! This crate demonstrates inter-crate dependencies within a workspace.

use workspace_core::{Config, compute, validate_input};

/// Extended configuration with additional utility features.
pub struct ExtendedConfig {
    pub core: Config,
    pub prefix: String,
}

impl ExtendedConfig {
    /// Create an extended config from a core config.
    pub fn from_core(core: Config, prefix: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        Self {
            core,
            prefix: prefix.into(),
        }
    }

    /// Returns a prefixed greeting.
    pub fn prefixed_greeting(&self) -> String {
        format!("[{}] {}", self.prefix, self.core.greeting())
    }
}

/// Batch compute operation using core's compute function.
pub fn batch_compute(pairs: &[(i32, i32)]) -> Vec<i32> {
    pairs.iter().map(|(a, b)| compute(*a, *b)).collect()
}

/// Process multiple inputs through validation.
pub fn process_inputs(inputs: &[&str]) -> Vec<Result<String, &'static str>> {
    inputs.iter().map(|input| validate_input(input)).collect()
}

/// Format a value with the workspace version.
pub fn format_with_version(value: &str) -> String {
    let config = Config::new("formatter");
    format!("{} ({})", value, config.version)
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_extended_config() {
        let core = Config::new("test");
        let extended = ExtendedConfig::from_core(core, "PREFIX");
        assert_eq!(extended.prefix, "PREFIX");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_prefixed_greeting() {
        let core = Config::new("app");
        let extended = ExtendedConfig::from_core(core, "INFO");
        let greeting = extended.prefixed_greeting();
        assert!(greeting.starts_with("[INFO]"));
        assert!(greeting.contains("app"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_batch_compute() {
        let pairs = vec![(1, 2), (3, 4), (5, 6)];
        let results = batch_compute(&pairs);
        assert_eq!(results, vec![3, 7, 11]);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_batch_compute_empty() {
        let results = batch_compute(&[]);
        assert!(results.is_empty());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_process_inputs() {
        let inputs = vec!["hello", "", "world"];
        let results = process_inputs(&inputs);
        assert_eq!(results.len(), 3);
        assert!(results[0].is_ok());
        assert!(results[1].is_err());
        assert!(results[2].is_ok());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_format_with_version() {
        let formatted = format_with_version("test value");
        assert!(formatted.contains("test value"));
        assert!(formatted.contains("0.1.0"));
    }
}
```

### crates/app/Cargo.toml

### crates/app/src/main.rs
```rust
//! Main application binary that uses both core and utils crates.
//! This demonstrates a complete workspace dependency chain: app -> utils -> core

use workspace_core::{Config, compute};
use workspace_utils::{ExtendedConfig, batch_compute, format_with_version};

fn main() {
    println!("=== RCH Workspace Fixture App ===");

    // Use core directly
    let config = Config::new("workspace_app");
    println!("Core greeting: {}", config.greeting());

    // Use utils (which internally uses core)
    let extended = ExtendedConfig::from_core(config.clone(), "APP");
    println!("Extended greeting: {}", extended.prefixed_greeting());

    // Demonstrate compute functions
    let sum = compute(10, 20);
    println!("Core compute(10, 20) = {}", sum);

    let batch_results = batch_compute(&[(1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 3)]);
    println!("Batch compute results: {:?}", batch_results);

    // Format with version
    let formatted = format_with_version("Application ready");
    println!("{}", formatted);

    println!("=== Fixture Test Complete ===");
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_app_uses_core() {
        let config = Config::new("test");
        assert_eq!(config.name, "test");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_app_uses_utils() {
        let core = Config::new("app");
        let extended = ExtendedConfig::from_core(core, "TEST");
        assert!(extended.prefixed_greeting().contains("[TEST]"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_dependency_chain() {
        // This test verifies the full dependency chain works
        let results = batch_compute(&[(5, 5)]);
        assert_eq!(results[0], 10);
    }
}
```

### tests/workspace_integration.rs
```rust
//! Integration tests that test the workspace as a whole.

use workspace_core::Config;
use workspace_utils::{ExtendedConfig, batch_compute};

#[test]
fn test_workspace_integration() {
    // Create config through core
    let config = Config::new("integration_test");

    // Extend it through utils
    let extended = ExtendedConfig::from_core(config, "INTEGRATION");

    // Verify the chain works
    let greeting = extended.prefixed_greeting();
    assert!(greeting.contains("[INTEGRATION]"));
    assert!(greeting.contains("integration_test"));
}

#[test]
fn test_cross_crate_computation() {
    // Use utils' batch_compute which internally uses core's compute
    let results = batch_compute(&[(100, 200), (0, 0), (-5, 5)]);
    assert_eq!(results, vec![300, 0, 0]);
}

#[test]
fn test_all_crates_accessible() {
    // Verify we can import from all workspace crates
    let _ = workspace_core::compute(1, 1);
    let _ = workspace_utils::format_with_version("test");
    // workspace_app is a binary, not importable
}
```

## Test Cases This Enables
1. cargo build --workspace - builds all members
2. cargo build -p workspace_app - builds only app (and dependencies)
3. cargo build -p workspace_core - builds only core (no deps)
4. cargo test --workspace - runs all workspace tests
5. cargo test -p workspace_utils - tests only utils
6. cargo check --workspace - checks all members
7. Incremental rebuild - change core, verify utils and app rebuild

## Unit Tests for Fixture Validation

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/workspace_fixture_tests.rs

use std::process::Command;
use std::path::PathBuf;

fn fixture_path() -> PathBuf {
    PathBuf::from("tests/e2e/fixtures/rust_workspace")
}

#[test]
fn test_workspace_fixture_structure() {
    let path = fixture_path();
    assert!(path.join("Cargo.toml").exists(), "Workspace Cargo.toml missing");
    assert!(path.join("crates/core/Cargo.toml").exists(), "core Cargo.toml missing");
    assert!(path.join("crates/core/src/lib.rs").exists(), "core lib.rs missing");
    assert!(path.join("crates/utils/Cargo.toml").exists(), "utils Cargo.toml missing");
    assert!(path.join("crates/utils/src/lib.rs").exists(), "utils lib.rs missing");
    assert!(path.join("crates/app/Cargo.toml").exists(), "app Cargo.toml missing");
    assert!(path.join("crates/app/src/main.rs").exists(), "app main.rs missing");
}

#[test]
fn test_workspace_builds() {
    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build", "--workspace"])
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output()
        .expect("cargo build failed");
    assert!(output.status.success(), "Workspace build failed: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));
}

#[test]
fn test_workspace_tests_pass() {
    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["test", "--workspace"])
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output()
        .expect("cargo test failed");
    assert!(output.status.success(), "Workspace tests failed: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout));
}

#[test]
fn test_package_specific_build() {
    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build", "-p", "workspace_core"])
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output()
        .expect("cargo build -p failed");
    assert!(output.status.success(), "Package build failed: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));
}

#[test]
fn test_package_specific_test() {
    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["test", "-p", "workspace_utils"])
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output()
        .expect("cargo test -p failed");
    assert!(output.status.success(), "Package test failed: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout));
}

#[test]
fn test_binary_output() {
    // Build first
    let _ = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build", "--workspace"])
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output();

    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["run", "-p", "workspace_app"])
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output()
        .expect("cargo run failed");

    assert!(output.status.success());
    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
    assert!(stdout.contains("RCH Workspace Fixture App"), "Expected app banner");
    assert!(stdout.contains("Fixture Test Complete"), "Expected completion message");
}

#[test]
fn test_workspace_check() {
    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["check", "--workspace"])
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output()
        .expect("cargo check failed");
    assert!(output.status.success(), "Workspace check failed: {}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));
}

#[test]
fn test_dependency_chain_correct() {
    // Build only app - should pull in core and utils
    let _ = Command::new("cargo")
        .arg("clean")
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output();

    let output = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["build", "-p", "workspace_app", "-v"])
        .current_dir(fixture_path())
        .output()
        .expect("cargo build failed");

    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
    // Verbose output should show compiling all three
    assert!(stderr.contains("workspace_core") || output.status.success());
    assert!(stderr.contains("workspace_utils") || output.status.success());
}
```
